You are the DEEP-THINKING DESIGNER for an autonomous research-report pipeline (POLARIS). Be adversarial, concrete, and cite file:line. READ-ONLY: do NOT edit files, do NOT run the pipeline, do NOT commit. There is a long pipeline run in flight — do not launch one.

REPO: /home/polaris/wt/flywheel (branch flywheel-v1).

BACKGROUND (established by a prior gate, trust but verify):
- The pipeline builds a literature review from a pre-built corpus of ~997 evidence rows (data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json), task: "restructuring impact of AI on the labor market".
- `route_orphan_baskets_to_section_plans` (src/polaris_graph/generator/verified_compose.py ~3784) routes every orphan claim-basket to a section; zero-lexical-overlap baskets land in a keep-all residual section "Additional Corroborated Findings".
- MEASURED: that residual absorbed **545 of 1032 evidence rows (53% of the corpus)**. An audit of those 545 found only ~195 carry any labor/AI/employment topical anchor; 56 are bot/captcha block pages; **~294 have NO topical anchor at all** — climate-risk IMF working papers, privacy-attack bibliographies, school infrastructure, content-marketing stats, Quora threads.
- CRITICAL FINDING: the §-1.3.1 off-topic DELETE leg fired **ZERO times** on the real run (logs/gate_baseline.log line ~1576: "DELETED 0 judge-confirmed off-topic item(s)"). The delete leg keys off `off_topic_ev_ids`, built by the caller (src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py ~10655-10683) from `is_row_deletable_offtopic` (src/polaris_graph/generator/junk_deletion_gate.py ~207-237), which requires an AFFIRMATIVE semantic topic-judge OFF_SUBJECT stamp on the row. **Nothing writes that stamp for a pre-built corpus** — the retrieval-time topic gate only DEMOTE-keeps. So the judge is effectively UNARMED and all the off-topic junk flows into the report's residual appendix.

THE OPERATOR-LOCKED RULES (read /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/CLAUDE.md §-1.3 and §-1.3.1, ~lines 55-75) — these are BINDING and a previous attempt to violate them cost a full day:
- WEIGHT, DON'T FILTER. CONSOLIDATE, DON'T DROP. Breadth/quality must EMERGE, never be forced.
- §-1.3.1 carve-out: ONLY two classes may be hard-DELETED — (a) chrome non-sources (captcha/404/empty), and (b) **semantically-confirmed off-topic sources, by SEMANTIC JUDGE VERDICT ONLY, FAIL-OPEN (any uncertainty => KEEP), NEVER by tier, lexical guess, or a breadth number.** Every deletion must be DISCLOSED (count + reason in Methods).
- Credible ON-TOPIC sources — even low-tier/social/non-journal — are NEVER deleted, only weighted.
- BANNED anti-pattern: adding caps/targets/thinners/hard-filters to make a breadth number go up.

YOUR TASK — design "Rank4: ARM THE SEMANTIC TOPIC JUDGE" so the ~294 genuinely off-topic rows are deleted-and-disclosed by JUDGE VERDICT, never by lexical guess. Answer with file:line evidence:

1. WHAT ALREADY EXISTS. Find every existing semantic topic-judge / relevance-judge / OFF_SUBJECT implementation in the repo (search for OFF_SUBJECT, topic_gate, topic_judge, off_subject, is_row_deletable_offtopic, and the cp4 "topic-judge dispositions" the F1 docstring mentions). For each: what does it do, what stamp/field does it write, who calls it, is it wired or dead code, and does it run at RETRIEVAL time only? Is there a judge I can simply ARM on a pre-built corpus, or must one be built? Quote the exact stamp field name(s) `is_row_deletable_offtopic` reads.

2. THE SEAM. Where exactly should the judge run for a PRE-BUILT corpus (the baseline path: scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py --corpus ...)? Give the precise call site. Note a prior fix already wired a junk gate into "the evidence_pool seam next to the retraction gate" — find it and say whether the topic judge belongs at the same seam.

3. COST / FEASIBILITY. ~997 rows to judge. What model does the pipeline use for judging (check the LLM client + any existing judge prompts)? Estimate calls/tokens/$ and wall-clock. Can rows be BATCHED per judge call? Is there caching/stamping so a second run is free? Is there an existing batched-judge helper to reuse?

4. FAIL-OPEN CORRECTNESS — the part that must not go wrong. The judge will be asked "is this source off-topic to the research question?". Design the exact contract so that: uncertainty/error/timeout/parse-failure => KEEP (never delete); a positive relevance signal VETOES deletion; only an affirmative, high-confidence OFF_SUBJECT verdict deletes. What are the failure modes that would cause a FALSE DELETE of a credible on-topic source (the thing §-1.3.1 most forbids)? Specifically: a seminal T1 paper (Acemoglu-Restrepo, Autor) whose title/abstract is generic, or a source whose fetched body is a stub. How do you make those SAFE? Should T1/T2 be exempt from deletion entirely, or does that violate "never by tier"? (Careful: "never delete BY tier" forbids tier as a DELETE trigger — does it forbid tier as a KEEP veto? Reason it out.)

5. DISCLOSURE. §-1.3.1 requires the deleted-row count + reason to reach Methods + the manifest, fail-loud. A prior fix found the junk_disclosed count was "dying on the result object" before reaching Methods. Find that plumbing and say exactly how the topic-judge deletions must be disclosed so they don't die the same way.

6. THE HONEST QUESTION. Is deleting these ~294 rows actually the right move for report QUALITY, or is the residual appendix already harmless? What measurably improves if they're gone — and what's the risk it makes things worse? Would it be better to keep them but stop them being CITED (weight-to-zero / exclude-from-bibliography), which is more conservative? Give a straight recommendation. If arming the judge is NOT the highest-value next step, say so and name what is.

Return: a concrete, minimal, kill-switched implementation plan (default-OFF, byte-identical when off), the exact fail-open contract, the disclosure plumbing, the cost estimate, and the traps. Prefer being right over being agreeable.

# POLARIS PROJECT - Operational Directives

CRITICAL: This file (CLAUDE.md) MUST be read in its entirety at the start of EVERY session and immediately following ANY restart. Failure to adhere to these directives constitutes a critical operational failure. See §3 for the mandatory startup protocol.

Document Class: Operational, Binding, Project-Specific
Scope: All POLARIS development, research pipeline implementation, and maintenance tasks.

---

## §-1. STANDING EVALUATION & DEBUG STANDARDS (binding for ALL work)

**These three standards override every other directive in this file when they conflict. §-1.1 and §-1.2 were flagged by user 2026-05-09 night as repeat-violation patterns; §-1.3 was operator-locked 2026-06-13 after a full day lost to symptom-patching.**

### §-1.1 Line-by-line audit standard (clinical-safety-critical)

Every evaluation, audit, comparison, benchmark, regression check, BEAT-BOTH framing, or quality assessment **MUST** be:

1. **Claim-by-claim** against the actually-fetched source content (cited span text, not title or abstract).
2. **Reasoning-step-by-reasoning-step** — verify each piece of reasoning follows from the cited evidence.
3. **Citation-by-citation** — verify each citation is appropriate for the claim.
4. **Apply industrial benchmarks** appropriate to the domain:
   - Clinical: PRISMA 2020, AMSTAR-2, GRADE per claim, ICMJE authorship/COI, ICH-GCP for trial methods, Cochrane RoB 2 / ROBINS-I / QUADAS-2.
   - Regulatory: jurisdiction-specific (FDA label, EMA SmPC, Health Canada PM, NICE TA, MHRA AR, TGA PI, PMDA review, NMPA labeling).
   - Methodology: appropriate risk-of-bias tool for the study type.
5. **Both Claude and Codex run independent line-by-line audits in parallel.** Cross-review combines findings.
6. Per-claim verdict: **VERIFIED / PARTIAL / UNSUPPORTED / FABRICATED / UNREACHABLE** with the specific cited span quote that supports the verdict.

**STRICTLY BANNED:**
- Word counts / citation counts / unique-source counts as quality signals
- Pattern presence ("does the report mention 'tirzepatide'?")
- Sample-based audits ("we audited 5 of 50 claims")
- String-presence PASS/FAIL checks (e.g., gate tables)
- Metadata comparison (e.g., "ChatGPT has 27 contradictions, POLARIS has 3, so ChatGPT is better" — this framing is **lethal in clinical context**)

**Why this matters:** clinical context. Pattern-matching evaluation will miss real fabrications. Patients can be hurt by a wrong dose, wrong contraindication, wrong indication population that survived a metadata check. "It is lethal" is literal.

**Application:** when the user asks for an audit/evaluation/comparison/benchmark, line-by-line is the ONLY acceptable answer. If you propose a metadata or pattern audit, you have failed the user.

### §-1.2 Standard debug workflow (no §3.0 bypass)

Every task / bug / issue follows this exact sequence — no shortcuts:

1. **GitHub Issue FIRST.** `gh issue create` BEFORE any branch, any code, any brief. Title format: `I-<prefix>-NNN — <summary>`. Body has acceptance criteria. **No exceptions.**
2. **Comprehensive grep/scan adjacent files.** Before writing the brief, grep all call sites, consumers, downstream rule checks, and tests touching the area. List them in the brief under **"Files I have ALSO checked and they're clean: [...]"**.
3. **Smoke test offline.** Unit tests run; if the change touches the pipeline, run a minimal smoke test (single sentence, single section) BEFORE launching a full sweep. Full sweeps are NOT smoke tests.
4. **Brief Codex.** Open with the iter-1 cap directive (§8.3.1 verbatim). Include the adjacent-file scan results so Codex VERIFIES rather than discovers.
5. **Goal: 1-2 iters per task, not 5.** The 5-cap is a backstop, not a target.
6. **If a big bug surfaces and cannot be resolved in 5 iters, mark it as URGENT new GitHub Issue and resolve it FIRST.** Do not let cap-5 force-approve a real production blocker.
7. **Close the GitHub Issue when the PR merges.** Do not leave resolved issues open.

**Ordering vs §3.1 boot ritual + §3.0 halt gates:** §-1.2 governs *task-work* tool calls. The §3.1 step-0 canonical-pin verification, CHARTER+PLAN SHA pins (§10), halt-marker check (`state/halt_*`), and any active halt gate ALWAYS run first. Only after those pass does the issue-driven sequence below kick in.

**Application:** for the assigned task, the FIRST *task-work* tool call (after boot ritual + halt checks) is `gh issue create` or `gh issue view`. The SECOND is comprehensive grep. The THIRD is offline smoke test. THEN brief Codex. Anything else is a §3.0 violation.

### §-1.3 Deep-research pipeline architecture DNA (operator-locked 2026-06-13)

**The pipeline is WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE, not FILTER-AND-CAP.** Locked by the operator after a full day was lost to symptom-patching the pipeline with hardcoded caps/targets/thinners to hit a breadth NUMBER. Three binding principles govern every retrieval/selection/composition design decision:

1. **WEIGHT, DON'T FILTER.** Every relevant source flows through to composition carrying a credibility **weight** (peer-review status, journal/venue reputation, gov / institute / news / social media). Do NOT hard-drop a source to hit a number. Social media STAYS at low weight — sometimes it reports a real journal. The tier classifier (T1–T7) and `authority_score` ARE the weighting system: use them as a per-citation **weight surfaced to the user**, never as a rank-then-drop hard filter.

2. **CONSOLIDATE, DON'T DROP.** Group sources that carry the SAME claim into a **basket**. Repetition is **corroboration** — the entire point of consolidate / distill / map-reduce. Multiple citations per claim is GOOD and expected. `finding_dedup` / `fact_dedup` must CONSOLIDATE (keep ALL sources per claim as multi-citation) and operate on **qualitative** claims too — never delete corroborating sources, never restrict to numeric-only.

3. **BASKET FAITHFULNESS.** Decide a claim's faithfulness against its **whole basket** of supporting sources, never a single URL/span — single-source verification is a blind spot. The verdict carries the corroboration (count + weights + agreement). This **STRENGTHENS** faithfulness; it NEVER relaxes it.

**THE ONLY HARD GATE is the faithfulness engine** (strict_verify / NLI entailment / 4-role D8 / provenance / span-grounding). Everything else is a WEIGHT or a CONSOLIDATION — never a DROP / CAP / THIN / TARGET.

**§-1.3.1 JUNK-DELETION CARVE-OUT (operator-authorized 2026-07-09, GH I-deepfix-003 #1374).** The never-DROP rule above is LIFTED for genuine JUNK. Rationale: weighting junk to zero still leaves it in the pool, bibliography, and corroboration counts, where it pollutes a clinical report. Two classes MAY be hard-DELETED from the corpus (not merely weight-0):
- **(a) Chrome non-sources** — bot/captcha pages, cookie/login/404/"not found" error pages, empty / no-usable-content rows. These are failed fetches, not sources.
- **(b) Semantically-confirmed OFF-TOPIC sources** — a whole source a SEMANTIC topic judge confirms is off-topic to the research question. Judge verdict ONLY, **FAIL-OPEN** (any uncertainty ⇒ KEEP), NEVER by tier, lexical guess, or a breadth number.

An off-topic SPAN inside an ON-TOPIC source is handled by dropping the SPAN from the output, keeping the source. Every deletion is **DISCLOSED** (deleted-row count + reason in Methods — fail loud, never silent).

**STILL BINDING (unchanged by this carve-out):** credible **ON-TOPIC** sources — even low-tier / social / non-journal — are NEVER deleted, only weighted (principle 1 holds for them; deletion must never touch a credible on-topic source). Duplicates CONSOLIDATE to one, never delete-all (principle 2). The faithfulness engine remains the only hard gate for CLAIMS (principle 3 / §-1.1). The day-waster ban still stands: deletion is triggered ONLY by content-integrity (chrome) or a semantic off-topic verdict — NEVER to hit a breadth or quality number.

**BANNED anti-pattern (the day-waster):** bolting hardcoded caps / targets / thinners / hard-filters onto the pipeline to make a breadth number go up (`PG_SPAN_PER_SOURCE_CITE_CAP`, `PG_LEGACY_SECTION_BREADTH_TARGET`, `PG_BREADTH_CANARY_MIN`, scope hard-filter, and similar). They FIGHT the architecture. Breadth and quality **EMERGE** from honest weighted multi-attribution; they are never forced. If you find yourself adding a knob to make a number hit a target — STOP. That is the bug.

**SURGICAL, NOT REWRITE.** The good machinery already exists — STORM retrieval, Zyte/crawl4ai/distill, the tier classifier (= weighting), `finding_dedup`/`fact_dedup` (= consolidation), the faithfulness engine. RE-WIRE it to the right semantics (weight not filter; consolidate-keep-all not drop; basket not single-span) and DELETE only the specific number-forcing bolt-ons. Do not over-kill.

Canonical detail lives in `docs/polaris_pipeline_canonical.md` and the forensic design doc `docs/pipeline_architecture_forensic_2026_06_13.md`. GitHub anchor: **I-arch-001 (#1245)**.

---

## §0. Core Identity and Operational Mode

**0.1 Identity:** You operate as a Senior Software Engineer/AI Agent building POLARIS - a production-grade research pipeline. Your responsibility is the high-fidelity implementation of reproducible, fully documented, and production-ready systems.

**0.2 Primary Directive:** Implement the Active Project Definition (APD) as defined in §1.1.
- **Rigor:** Execute without omission, ad-hoc simplification, or corner-cutting. Do not be sloppy. Do not simplify. Do not silently degrade functionality or quality.
- **Exploration:** During exploratory phases where the APD is intentionally broad, the directive is to clarify intent, deliver high-value iterations, and rigorously document findings and the evolution of the scope (LAW I).

**0.3 Operational Mode (Mandatory Extended Thinking):** You must always engage extended thinking capabilities for planning. Every task requires this rigorous process:
1. **Analyze:** Deconstruct the request and synthesize the current APD (§1.1).
2. **Research:** Proactively search online for the latest best practices, libraries, and solutions (LAW III).
3. **Plan:** Formulate a detailed, step-by-step execution plan, considering edge cases and alternatives.
4. **Execute:** Implement the plan rigorously, ensuring synchronization (LAW I).
5. **Verify:** Validate the output against requirements and log the evidence (LAW II).
6. **Record:** Update all mandatory documentation and state files (§2).

The reasoning derived from steps 1-3 MUST be captured in the RATIONALE section of the session log (§2.2).

---

## §1. The LAWS (Non-Negotiable Directives)

These LAWS supersede all other instructions.

### LAW I — Active Definition and Synchronization
You must adhere strictly to the Active Project Definition (APD) and ensure its components are coherent.

**1.1 Active Project Definition (APD) Hierarchy** (updated 2026-05-05 per polaris-restart Plan §9.1 issue-driven workflow): The APD is the dynamic source of truth for the project's current goals. It is derived, in order of precedence, from:
1. Current Session Instructions: Explicit requests and clarifications made during the active session.
2. `state/active_issue.json` (The Active Work): currently in_progress GitHub Issue per `state/polaris_restart/issue_breakdown.md`. Cannot start Issue N+1 until Issue N completed.
3. `state/polaris_restart/issue_breakdown.md` (The Scope): 134 issues, Codex APPROVE iter 4 on 2026-05-05. Per-Issue GREEN criteria. Authoritative for what's "done" in issue-driven workflow.
4. `state/polaris_restart/plan.md` (The Restart Plan): Codex APPROVE iter 4. Defines role split, halt conditions, mechanical gates.
5. `docs/carney_delivery_plan_v6_2.md` (The Long-term Mission): canonical 18-week plan, hash-pinned via `docs/canonical_pin.txt`. Re-read at every session-resume.
6. Recent `logs/session_log.md` (The History).
7. `architecture.md` (The Baseline).
8. `docs/task_acceptance_matrix.yaml` (HISTORICAL — Plan-v13-era pre-Issue tracking; matrix-decommission scheduled as post-Cleanup-PR-8 follow-up PR).

**1.2 Mandatory Synchronization:** If a higher-priority item contradicts a lower-priority item, the lower-priority documentation MUST be updated immediately to reflect the higher-priority truth. This synchronization must occur before execution proceeds and must be logged (§2.2).

641:                if v == "OFF_SUBJECT":
686:        # I-deepfix-003 #1374 Fix 3: stamp the deletable sidecar ONLY on OFF_SUBJECT
691:            row["topic_off_subject"] = True
693:        # re-judged NON-OFF_SUBJECT (confident-ON or OFF_ASPECT). The sidecar was only ever SET
694:        # True (above) and never cleared, so a STALE topic_off_subject=True reloaded from an
696:        # — run_honest_sweep_r3 builds its fresh OFF_SUBJECT id set from ``demoted_rows where
697:        # topic_off_subject is True``, so that stale row entered the set and was deleted as
701:        # present) — so a fresh non-OFF_SUBJECT row stays sidecar-ABSENT (the contract downstream
706:                row.pop("topic_off_subject", None)
708:                row.pop("topic_off_subject", None)
714:        f"topic_gate: in={n_in} kept={len(kept_rows)} "
721:            "[scope] topic_gate %s %d off-topic source(s): %s",

1	"""
2	Semantic topic-relevance gate — I-scope-001 (#1244).
3	
4	WHY THIS EXISTS (grounded diagnosis, do not re-derive):
5	A consolidated drb_72 breadth run cited 76 distinct sources with 0
6	fabrication, but ~9 were contamination — including 4 OFF-TOPIC-but-CREDIBLE
7	journals (spinal-cord stimulation, blockchain-sustainability, etc.). The
8	tier system rates CREDIBILITY, not RELEVANCE, so a credible-but-irrelevant
9	journal passes the tier gate. The lexical/embedding relevance floor ALSO
10	fails: en.wikipedia.org scored 0.583 relevance (above the clean median
11	0.500) because contaminants share generic content words with on-topic
12	sources. So neither tier nor embedding similarity can separate
13	off-topic-credible from on-topic — a TOPIC gate (semantic ON/OFF judgement
14	on the research-question domain) is required.
15	
16	This module is the pure, LLM-based topic gate. It is DEFAULT-OFF: the
17	orchestrator (run_honest_sweep_r3.py) only calls it when PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_GATE
18	is truthy, and passes the production LLM callable + batch size. Keeping all
19	batching / parsing / exemption logic here (pure) means the gate is fully
20	unit-testable with a stub `llm_callable`, with NO OpenRouter key required.
21	
22	FAITHFULNESS LOCK: this gate is SELECTION-SIDE ONLY. It can only SUBTRACT a
23	candidate source from the pool handed to the generator; it NEVER edits a
24	sentence, span, or citation. strict_verify / the NLI entailment judge / the
25	4-role D8 audit / provenance are UNTOUCHED — every surviving sentence still
26	passes the identical faithfulness stack. Subtraction cannot fabricate.
27	
28	FAIL-OPEN CONTRACT (LAW II — never drop on uncertainty):
29	The gate drops a source ONLY on an explicit, confident OFF verdict. It KEEPS
30	the source (fail-open) on:
31	  - any LLM exception,
32	  - a returned verdict count that does not match the requested count,
33	  - any unparseable / unrecognised verdict line,
34	  - an empty / missing title+snippet (nothing to judge on).
35	A marquee / required-entity anchor is NEVER dropped, regardless of verdict.
36	"""

154	    (bot/captcha/cookie/404/login/empty). Reads a pre-existing ``content_integrity_junk``

158	    normally — a detector bug must never silently skip a real source)."""

166	            detect_content_integrity_junk as _detect_ci_junk,

173	        title = str(row.get("title") or row.get("source_title") or row.get("statement") or "")

261	def _build_batch_prompt(
262	    research_question: str,
263	    batch: list[tuple[int, str, str]],
264	    *,
265	    subject_aspect_split: bool = False,
266	) -> str:
267	    """Build a single ON/OFF-topic classification prompt for a batch of
268	    sources. ``batch`` is a list of (local_index, title, snippet). The LLM is
269	    asked to return exactly one line per source: ``<index>: ON`` or
270	    ``<index>: OFF``. Confident-OFF-only is enforced at parse time.
271	
272	    ``subject_aspect_split`` (I-deepfix-003 #1374 Fix 3): when True the OFF verdict
273	    is split into ``OFF_ASPECT`` (same subject, wrong aspect — kept/demoted) and
274	    ``OFF_SUBJECT`` (clearly different subject — the only deletable OFF). When False
275	    (the byte-identical legacy) the prompt asks only for ``ON`` / ``OFF``."""
276	    # I-deepfix-003 #1374 Fix 3: the STEP-2 rubric, the domain-neutral example, the
277	    # OUTPUT CONTRACT, and the trailer are the ONLY segments that differ between the
278	    # legacy two-verdict form and the three-verdict subject/aspect split. Everything
279	    # else (STEP 1, the date-blind rule, the fail-open) is byte-identical in both. The
280	    # ``else`` strings below reproduce the legacy prompt EXACTLY (byte-identical OFF).
281	    if subject_aspect_split:
282	        step2_line = (
283	            "STEP 2 — for EACH numbered source below, choose EXACTLY ONE verdict: "
284	            "ON if the source plausibly bears on BOTH the subject entity AND that "
285	            "specific aspect; OFF_ASPECT if the source is about the SAME subject "
286	            "entity but a DIFFERENT aspect / use-case / population than the question "
287	            "asks about (same subject, wrong question — a topic-adjacent hub that is "
288	            "KEPT and only demoted, never removed); OFF_SUBJECT if the source is "
289	            "about a CLEARLY DIFFERENT subject entity (different field, disease, "
290	            "domain — e.g. an unrelated-domain or scholar-mill paper). When you are "
291	            "unsure between OFF_ASPECT and OFF_SUBJECT, choose OFF_ASPECT (the "
292	            "safer, keep-and-demote verdict)."
293	        )
294	        example_line = (
295	            "Example (domain-neutral): if the question is about entity X and aspect "
296	            "A, then a source about entity X but aspect B is OFF_ASPECT; a source "
297	            "about a clearly different entity Y is OFF_SUBJECT; a source about entity "
298	            "X and aspect A is ON."
299	        )
300	        output_contract_line = (
301	            "OUTPUT CONTRACT (strict — the parser accepts nothing else): OUTPUT ONLY "
302	            "THE VERDICT LINES, exactly one per source, each line EXACTLY in the form "
303	            "`<index>: ON`, `<index>: OFF_ASPECT`, or `<index>: OFF_SUBJECT`. Do NOT "
304	            "write the entity or aspect names, any reasoning, any explanation, or any "
305	            "other words — not on a verdict line and not anywhere else in the output."
306	        )
307	        trailer_line = (
308	            "VERDICTS (one `<index>: ON|OFF_ASPECT|OFF_SUBJECT` line per source):"
309	        )
310	    else:
311	        step2_line = (
312	            "STEP 2 — for EACH numbered source below, mark it ON only if it plausibly "
313	            "bears on BOTH the subject entity AND that specific aspect. A source about "
314	            "the SAME entity but a DIFFERENT aspect / use-case / population than the "
315	            "question asks about is OFF-TOPIC — same subject, wrong question. A source "
316	            "about a clearly different subject entity (different field, disease, "
317	            "population) is also OFF-TOPIC."
318	        )
319	        example_line = (
320	            "Example (domain-neutral): if the question is about entity X and aspect A, "
321	            "then a source about entity X but aspect B is OFF; a source about entity X "
322	            "and aspect A is ON."
323	        )
324	        output_contract_line = (
325	            "OUTPUT CONTRACT (strict — the parser accepts nothing else): OUTPUT ONLY "
326	            "THE VERDICT LINES, exactly one per source, each line EXACTLY in the form "
327	            "`<index>: ON` or `<index>: OFF`. Do NOT write the entity or aspect names, "
328	            "any reasoning, any explanation, or any other words — not on a verdict "
329	            "line and not anywhere else in the output."
330	        )
331	        trailer_line = "VERDICTS (one `<index>: ON|OFF` line per source):"
332	    lines = [
333	        "You are a strict topic-relevance classifier for a research report.",
334	        "",
335	        f"RESEARCH QUESTION:\n{research_question.strip()}",
336	        "",
337	        # I-deepfix-001 FF4-ASPECT (v2, forensic-corrected): FACET-SCOPED rubric
338	        # (was entity-only) + a STRICT verdict-only output contract. An
339	        # entity-only "different subject?" prompt is structurally blind to a
340	        # same-entity / wrong-ASPECT source (e.g. a GenAI-in-education paper
341	        # grounding a GenAI-labor-market question): the shared entity satisfies
342	        # "not a different field" and the fail-open keeps it ON. The two-step
343	        # rubric below makes the model name the question's SUBJECT ENTITY *and*
344	        # its SPECIFIC ASPECT *silently* (internal reasoning only), then requires
345	        # a source to bear on BOTH. It is domain-agnostic (LAW VI — the aspect is
346	        # derived at runtime from the RESEARCH QUESTION; nothing domain-specific
347	        # is hardcoded) and preserves the explicit fail-open.
348	        #
349	        # FORENSIC ADJUSTMENT #2 (off-topic fix_change_needed): the entity+aspect
350	        # naming MUST stay INTERNAL and the output MUST be strictly one
351	        # `<index>: ON|OFF` line per source and nothing else. Inline reasoning on
352	        # a verdict line ("1: this is about L2 writing, OFF") is not recognised by
353	        # _parse_batch_verdicts (below, unchanged) -> count mismatch -> the WHOLE
354	        # batch fails OPEN, silently letting the off-aspect source survive. So the
355	        # output contract is hardened here rather than the parser being relaxed.
356	        "STEP 1 (do this SILENTLY — this reasoning must NOT appear anywhere in "
357	        "your output): read the RESEARCH QUESTION and name to yourself its two "
358	        "parts — the SUBJECT ENTITY it is about, and the SPECIFIC ASPECT it asks "
359	        "about that entity (the outcome, relation, sub-domain, use-case, or "
360	        "population the question is actually asking about).",
361	        "",
362	        step2_line,
363	        "",
364	        example_line,
365	        "",
366	        # I-deepfix-001 (drb_72 forensic): the seminal on-topic papers were wrongly marked OFF
367	        # because the question text embeds a DATE window ("before June 2023") and the judge read a
368	        # post-cutoff date/marker in a snippet as a "different aspect". TOPICALITY IS DATE-BLIND.
369	        "TOPICALITY IS DATE-BLIND: a publication date — or ANY date in the research question — is "
370	        "NOT an aspect. NEVER mark a source OFF because of its publication date or a date range in "
371	        "the question; recency is a SEPARATE axis handled elsewhere. Judge ONLY subject-entity + "
372	        "aspect. An exposure / projection / potential-impact / early-evidence study of the "
373	        "question's aspect is ON (it bears on that aspect), not OFF.",
374	        "",
375	        "FAIL-OPEN: if you genuinely cannot tell whether the source addresses the "
376	        "question's specific aspect, mark it ON. When in doubt, answer ON.",
377	        "",
378	        output_contract_line,
379	        "",
380	        "SOURCES:",
381	    ]
382	    for local_idx, title, snippet in batch:
383	        text = title.strip()
384	        if snippet:
385	            text = f"{text} — {snippet}" if text else snippet
386	        if not text:
387	            text = "(no title or snippet)"
388	        lines.append(f"{local_idx}: {text}")
389	    lines.append("")
390	    lines.append(trailer_line)
391	    return "\n".join(lines)

394	def _parse_batch_verdicts(
395	    raw: str,
396	    expected_indices: list[int],
397	) -> dict[int, bool] | None:
398	    """Parse the LLM batch response into ``{local_index: is_offtopic}``.
399	
400	    Returns None (FAIL-OPEN signal — keep the whole batch) when the parse is
401	    not exactly one recognised verdict per requested index. A recognised
402	    verdict line is ``<index>: ON`` or ``<index>: OFF`` (case-insensitive,
403	    tolerant of surrounding punctuation). Anything else => fail-open."""
404	    if not isinstance(raw, str) or not raw.strip():
405	        return None
406	    verdicts: dict[int, bool] = {}
407	    wanted = set(expected_indices)
408	    for line in raw.splitlines():
409	        stripped = line.strip()
410	        if not stripped or ":" not in stripped:
411	            continue
412	        idx_part, _, verdict_part = stripped.partition(":")
413	        idx_token = idx_part.strip().lstrip("-").strip()
414	        if not idx_token.isdigit():
415	            continue
416	        idx = int(idx_token)
417	        if idx not in wanted:
418	            continue
419	        verdict_token = verdict_part.strip().lower()
420	        # Confident ON / confident OFF only. Anything ambiguous is ignored
421	        # (so the count check below will trip fail-open).
422	        if verdict_token.startswith("on"):
423	            verdicts[idx] = False
424	        elif verdict_token.startswith("off"):
425	            verdicts[idx] = True
426	        # else: leave unset -> count mismatch -> fail-open
427	    if set(verdicts.keys()) != wanted:
428	        # Missing / extra / unparseable verdicts: keep the whole batch.
429	        return None
430	    return verdicts

434	    raw: str,
435	    expected_indices: list[int],
436	) -> dict[int, str] | None:
437	    """Parse the three-verdict (subject/aspect split) LLM response into
438	    ``{local_index: "ON" | "OFF_ASPECT" | "OFF_SUBJECT"}``.
439	
440	    Returns None (FAIL-OPEN — keep the whole batch) on the SAME conditions as the
441	    legacy :func:`_parse_batch_verdicts`: empty/blank input, or any result that is
442	    not exactly one recognised verdict per requested index. Recognised verdict
443	    tokens (case-insensitive, separator-tolerant so ``off subject`` / ``off-subject``
444	    / ``off_subject`` all match): ``on`` -> ON, ``off_subject`` -> OFF_SUBJECT,
445	    ``off_aspect`` -> OFF_ASPECT, and a legacy bare ``off`` -> OFF_ASPECT
446	    (conservative — the old verdict form is NEVER treated as deletable). Anything
447	    unrecognised is ignored so the count check below trips fail-open."""
448	    if not isinstance(raw, str) or not raw.strip():
449	        return None
450	    verdicts: dict[int, str] = {}
451	    wanted = set(expected_indices)
452	    for line in raw.splitlines():
453	        stripped = line.strip()
454	        if not stripped or ":" not in stripped:
455	            continue
456	        idx_part, _, verdict_part = stripped.partition(":")
457	        idx_token = idx_part.strip().lstrip("-").strip()
458	        if not idx_token.isdigit():
459	            continue
460	        idx = int(idx_token)
461	        if idx not in wanted:
462	            continue
463	        # Normalise separators so "off subject"/"off-subject"/"off_subject" collapse.
464	        norm = verdict_part.strip().lower().replace("-", "_").replace(" ", "_")
465	        if norm.startswith("on"):
466	            verdicts[idx] = "ON"
467	        elif norm.startswith("off_subject") or norm.startswith("offsubject"):
468	            verdicts[idx] = "OFF_SUBJECT"
469	        elif norm.startswith("off_aspect") or norm.startswith("offaspect"):
470	            verdicts[idx] = "OFF_ASPECT"
471	        elif norm.startswith("off"):
472	            # Legacy bare OFF -> OFF_ASPECT (conservative: never delete on the
473	            # old verdict form).
474	            verdicts[idx] = "OFF_ASPECT"
475	        # else: unrecognised -> leave unset -> count mismatch -> fail-open.
476	    if set(verdicts.keys()) != wanted:
477	        return None
478	    return verdicts
479

481	def classify_topic_relevance(
482	    sources: list[dict[str, Any]],
483	    research_question: str,
484	    llm_callable: Callable[[str], str],
485	    *,
486	    batch_size: int | None = None,
487	    primary_trial_anchors: list[str] | None = None,
488	    anchor_predicate: Callable[[dict[str, Any]], bool] | None = None,
489	) -> TopicGateResult:
490	    """Drop sources CONFIDENTLY classified OFF-topic for the research question.
491	
492	    Pure + side-effect-free (apart from logging): the caller supplies the LLM
493	    via ``llm_callable(prompt: str) -> str`` so this is fully unit-testable
494	    with a stub. Marquee / required-entity anchors are EXEMPT (never dropped).
495	    FAIL-OPEN: any LLM exception, count mismatch, or unparseable verdict keeps
496	    the whole batch. Drops ONLY on an explicit confident OFF verdict.
497	
498	    Args:
499	        sources: evidence rows (selection-stage, post floor + dedup).
500	        research_question: the user's raw question (the topic anchor).
501	        llm_callable: synchronous ``str -> str`` LLM interface.
502	        batch_size: sources per LLM call (default :func:`topic_batch_size`).
503	        primary_trial_anchors: named-trial anchors; a row matching one is
504	            exempt (handled via ``anchor_predicate`` when supplied).
505	        anchor_predicate: optional extra "is this a primary anchor" test
506	            (the orchestrator passes the selector's anchor matcher so the
507	            exemption is identical to the floor stage). Marquee detection is
508	            always applied in addition.
509	
510	    Returns:
511	        TopicGateResult with kept/dropped rows + honest telemetry.
512	    """
513	    n_in = len(sources)
514	    if n_in == 0:
515	        return TopicGateResult(
516	            kept_rows=[], dropped_rows=[], n_in=0, n_kept=0,
517	            n_dropped_offtopic=0, notes=["topic_gate: empty pool"],
518	        )
519	    if not (research_question or "").strip():
520	        # Nothing to anchor on — FAIL-OPEN, keep everything.
521	        return TopicGateResult(
522	            kept_rows=list(sources), dropped_rows=[], n_in=n_in,
523	            n_kept=n_in, n_dropped_offtopic=0,
524	            notes=["topic_gate: empty research_question — fail-open"],
525	        )

527	    size = batch_size if (batch_size and batch_size > 0) else topic_batch_size()
528	
529	    def _is_exempt(row: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
530	        if _row_is_marquee_anchor(row):

532	        if anchor_predicate is not None:
533	            try:
534	                return bool(anchor_predicate(row))
535	            except Exception:
536	                return False
537	        return False
538	
539	    # Partition exempt rows out — they bypass classification entirely.
540	    exempt_rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
541	    judged_rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
542	    judged_meta: list[tuple[str, str]] = []  # (title, snippet) per judged row
543	    # I-deepfix-003 (#1374) Fix 4 (topic-side): chrome non-sources are NOT judged (nothing real
544	    # to judge) — tracked separately so they never inflate the marquee-``exempt`` telemetry, and
545	    # they stay in ``sources`` so the DEFAULT keep-all path still returns them (the chrome-delete
546	    # leg removes them later). Read the flag ONCE.
547	    chrome_skip = junk_chrome_before_offtopic_enabled()

549	    for row in sources:
550	        if _is_exempt(row):

552	            continue

554	            # A failed fetch, not a source — never judge it, never stamp it off-topic.
555	            n_chrome_skipped += 1
556	            continue
557	        title = _row_title_text(row)
558	        snippet = _row_snippet_text(row)
559	        if not title and not snippet:
560	            # Nothing to judge on -> keep (fail-open per-row).
561	            exempt_rows.append(row)
562	            continue
563	        judged_rows.append(row)
564	        judged_meta.append((title, snippet))

566	    # Confident-OFF verdicts are accumulated in the loop. The kept set is
567	    # computed once below preserving the caller's ORIGINAL order — critical
568	    # because `evidence_for_gen` arrives already ranked best-first (relevance x
569	    # authority) and there is NO re-rank between this gate and the generator.
570	    # Partitioning exempt/kept to the end would push high-value marquee / anchor
571	    # rows to the tail of the list the generator sees (a real regression on the
572	    # gate-ON acceptance path).
573	    #
574	    # I-deepfix-001 (§-1.3 WEIGHT, DON'T FILTER — the operator names the "scope
575	    # hard-filter" as a BANNED anti-pattern; the ONLY hard gate is the
576	    # faithfulness engine): a confident-OFF source is, BY DEFAULT, KEPT in the
577	    # pool and DEMOTED (disclosed off-topic via the ``topic_offtopic_demoted``
578	    # sidecar), NEVER hard-dropped. Topicality is a WEIGHT — the source still
579	    # flows to composition where the UNCHANGED strict_verify / NLI / 4-role
580	    # engine is the only gate. The legacy hard-drop is preserved behind
581	    # ``PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_GATE_HARD_DROP`` (audit / reversal). Order is unchanged in
582	    # BOTH modes (no tail-partition), so a demoted row stays best-first-ranked.

585	        "0", "false", "no", "off",
586	    )
587	    # I-deepfix-003 #1374 Fix 3: split the OFF verdict into OFF_ASPECT (demote-keep) vs
588	    # OFF_SUBJECT (the only deletable OFF). Default ON; OFF = byte-identical legacy prompt
589	    # + two-verdict parser + no ``topic_off_subject`` sidecar.
590	    split = topic_gate_subject_aspect_split_enabled()
591	    offtopic_rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
592	    offtopic_titles: list[str] = []
593	    ontopic_rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []  # I-deepfix-001: confident-ON, for the rescue False-stamp

595	    # ONLY rows that receive the deletable ``topic_off_subject=True`` sidecar. Empty
596	    # unless the subject/aspect split is enabled.
597	    offsubject_rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
598	    # I-deepfix-003 gate-fix (Codex P1): confident-OFF_ASPECT rows (same entity, wrong
599	    # aspect — demote-KEEP, NEVER deletable). Tracked so the deletable ``topic_off_subject``
600	    # sidecar can be CLEARED to False on them (demote path below) — a STALE True baked into
601	    # corpus_snapshot by an earlier run must NEVER survive on a row THIS run re-verdicts
602	    # OFF_ASPECT (it would be misread as a fresh OFF_SUBJECT and deleted). Empty unless the
603	    # subject/aspect split is enabled.
604	    offaspect_rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
605	
606	    for start in range(0, len(judged_rows), size):
607	        end = min(start + size, len(judged_rows))
608	        batch_rows = judged_rows[start:end]
609	        batch_meta = judged_meta[start:end]
610	        batch = [
611	            (local_idx, batch_meta[local_idx][0], batch_meta[local_idx][1])
612	            for local_idx in range(len(batch_rows))
613	        ]
614	        expected = [b[0] for b in batch]
615	        prompt = _build_batch_prompt(
616	            research_question, batch, subject_aspect_split=split,
617	        )
618	        try:
619	            raw = llm_callable(prompt)
620	        except Exception as exc:  # FAIL-OPEN on any LLM error -> keep batch.
621	            _LOGGER.warning(
622	                "[scope] topic_gate batch LLM error — fail-open, keeping "
623	                "%d sources: %s", len(batch_rows), str(exc)[:200],
624	            )
625	            continue
626	        if split:
627	            verdicts = _parse_batch_verdicts_split(raw, expected)
628	        else:
629	            verdicts = _parse_batch_verdicts(raw, expected)
630	        if verdicts is None:  # FAIL-OPEN on count mismatch / unparseable.
631	            _LOGGER.warning(
632	                "[scope] topic_gate batch unparseable / count mismatch — "
633	                "fail-open, keeping %d sources", len(batch_rows),
634	            )
635	            continue
636	        for local_idx, row in enumerate(batch_rows):
637	            v = verdicts.get(local_idx)
638	            if split:
639	                # Three-verdict split. Both OFF kinds are DEMOTED (weight); only
640	                # OFF_SUBJECT additionally carries the deletable sidecar below.
641	                if v == "OFF_SUBJECT":
642	                    offtopic_rows.append(row)
643	                    offtopic_titles.append(batch_meta[local_idx][0] or "(no title)")
644	                    offsubject_rows.append(row)
645	                elif v == "OFF_ASPECT":
646	                    offtopic_rows.append(row)
647	                    offtopic_titles.append(batch_meta[local_idx][0] or "(no title)")
648	                    offaspect_rows.append(row)
649	                elif v == "ON":
650	                    ontopic_rows.append(row)
651	                continue
652	            if v is True:  # confident OFF only
653	                offtopic_rows.append(row)
654	                # batch_meta is already the per-batch slice -> index locally.
655	                offtopic_titles.append(batch_meta[local_idx][0] or "(no title)")
656	            elif v is False:  # confident ON
657	                # I-deepfix-001 (drb_72 forensic): RESCUE semantics. Historically the gate wrote
658	                # ONLY True (never False), so a re-judge (PG_RESUME_RUN_TOPIC_JUDGE) could ADD demotes
659	                # but never CLEAR a stale bad stamp baked into the corpus_snapshot by an earlier run.
660	                # Stamping False on a confident-ON verdict lets a fixed-prompt re-judge un-bury the
661	                # seminal papers wrongly demoted upstream. Faithfulness-neutral: downstream
662	                # (_is_confirmed_offtopic keys on `is True`; is_topic_unjudged on `is not None`) already
663	                # handles False. Gated PG_TOPIC_GATE_RESCUE_ON_STAMP (default ON); OFF = byte-identical.
664	                ontopic_rows.append(row)
665	
666	    if hard_drop:
667	        # LEGACY (explicit opt-in): hard-drop the confident-OFF set.
668	        _off_ids = {id(r) for r in offtopic_rows}
669	        kept_rows = [r for r in sources if id(r) not in _off_ids]
670	        dropped_rows, dropped_titles = offtopic_rows, offtopic_titles
671	        demoted_rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
672	        demoted_titles: list[str] = []
673	    else:
674	        # DEFAULT (§-1.3 keep-all + demote): KEEP every source (original order),
675	        # disclose the confident-OFF set as DEMOTED, and stamp a faithfulness-
676	        # neutral sidecar so a downstream weighter can sink it WITHOUT dropping.
677	        kept_rows = list(sources)
678	        for row in offtopic_rows:
679	            row["topic_offtopic_demoted"] = True
680	        # I-deepfix-001 (drb_72): RESCUE — stamp False on confident-ON rows so a fixed-prompt
681	        # re-judge CLEARS a stale bad True baked into the corpus_snapshot by an earlier run.
682	        # Gated PG_TOPIC_GATE_RESCUE_ON_STAMP (default ON); OFF => no False stamp = byte-identical.
683	        if rescue_on_stamp:
684	            for row in ontopic_rows:
685	                row["topic_offtopic_demoted"] = False
686	        # I-deepfix-003 #1374 Fix 3: stamp the deletable sidecar ONLY on OFF_SUBJECT
687	        # rows (a clearly different subject — scholar-mill / unrelated-domain junk).
688	        # OFF_ASPECT rows carry ONLY ``topic_offtopic_demoted`` (demote-keep, never
689	        # deletable). The downstream junk-deletion gate keys deletion on this sidecar.
690	        for row in offsubject_rows:
691	            row["topic_off_subject"] = True
692	        # I-deepfix-003 gate-fix (Codex P1): POP the deletable sidecar off every row THIS run

700	        # ``pop(..., None)`` REMOVES a stale True but is a no-op on a clean row (key never
701	        # present) — so a fresh non-OFF_SUBJECT row stays sidecar-ABSENT (the contract downstream

703	        # PG_TOPIC_GATE_SUBJECT_ASPECT_SPLIT=0 is byte-identical (legacy path never writes it).
704	        if split:
705	            for row in offaspect_rows:
706	                row.pop("topic_off_subject", None)
707	            for row in ontopic_rows:
708	                row.pop("topic_off_subject", None)

710	        demoted_rows, demoted_titles = offtopic_rows, offtopic_titles
711	
712	    verb = "dropped_offtopic" if hard_drop else "demoted_offtopic"
713	    notes = [
714	        f"topic_gate: in={n_in} kept={len(kept_rows)} "
715	        f"{verb}={len(offtopic_rows)} exempt={len(exempt_rows)} "
716	        f"chrome_skipped={n_chrome_skipped} "
717	        f"batch_size={size}"
718	    ]
719	    if offtopic_titles:
720	        _LOGGER.info(
721	            "[scope] topic_gate %s %d off-topic source(s): %s",
722	            "DROPPED" if hard_drop else "DEMOTED(kept, disclosed)",
723	            len(offtopic_titles),
724	            "; ".join(t[:120] for t in offtopic_titles),
725	        )
726	
727	    return TopicGateResult(
728	        kept_rows=kept_rows,
729	        dropped_rows=dropped_rows,
730	        dropped_titles=dropped_titles,
731	        n_in=n_in,
732	        n_kept=len(kept_rows),
733	        n_dropped_offtopic=len(dropped_rows),
734	        n_exempt=len(exempt_rows),
735	        notes=notes,
736	        demoted_rows=demoted_rows,
737	        demoted_titles=demoted_titles,
738	        n_demoted_offtopic=len(demoted_rows),
739	    )

1	"""GH I-deepfix-003 (#1374) — junk-deletion grounding gate.
2	
3	Operator rule change 2026-07-09 (CLAUDE.md §-1.3.1): the §-1.3 never-DROP rule is
4	LIFTED for genuine JUNK. Weighting junk to zero is insufficient — a zero-weight row
5	still sits in the evidence pool, the bibliography, and the per-claim corroboration
6	counts, where it pollutes a clinical report (a bot-detection page anchoring a claim; a
7	predatory PDF padding the count; an off-topic law/tourism source). Two classes are
8	hard-DELETED from the run-level grounding pool BEFORE generation:
9	
10	  (a) CHROME NON-SOURCES — bot/captcha/cookie/404/login/empty pages. A failed fetch is
11	      not a source. The content-integrity detector stamps ``row["content_integrity_junk"]``
12	      upstream (the re-tier seam); this gate deletes the stamped rows.
13	  (b) SEMANTICALLY-CONFIRMED OFF-TOPIC whole sources — a source a SEMANTIC topic judge
14	      confirmed off-topic to the research question. DEFAULT path (Fix 1,
15	      ``is_row_deletable_offtopic``) deletes ONLY on the topic judge's affirmative
16	      OFF_SUBJECT stamp: a weight/reranker ``content_relevance_label``
17	      (``demoted`` / ``escalated_demoted``) can NEVER delete, and a positive relevance
18	      verdict (``relevant`` / ``escalated_relevant``) vetoes deletion UNCONDITIONALLY. Judge
19	      verdict ONLY, FAIL-OPEN (any uncertainty / missing verdict / error => KEEP), never a
20	      lexical/tier/number rule. The legacy weight-label reuse
21	      (``weighted_enrichment._is_confirmed_offtopic`` via ``is_row_confirmed_offtopic``) is
22	      reachable only behind ``PG_DELETE_OFFTOPIC_TOPIC_JUDGE_ONLY=0`` (byte-identical OFF).
23	
24	STILL BINDING (§-1.3): credible ON-TOPIC sources, even low-tier / social / non-journal,
25	are NEVER deleted here (they are neither content-junk nor confirmed-off-topic). An
26	off-topic SPAN inside an on-topic source is handled at compose (the span screen), not
27	here. Duplicates consolidate elsewhere. The faithfulness engine is UNTOUCHED — deleting a
28	row can only SHRINK the grounding pool, so a claim citing a deleted junk row FAILS
29	strict_verify (it can never make one PASS). A deleted junk row was never a valid anchor.
30	
31	MARQUEE/CONTRACT EXEMPTION: a required-entity / contract-bound anchor is on-topic by
32	construction and is NEVER deleted (its evidence_id is in ``exempt_ids``), mirroring the
33	topic gate's existing anchor exemption.
34	
35	Deletion is DISCLOSED: every deleted row is RETURNED with its reason for the manifest + a
36	LOUD log + a Methods line (fail loud, never silent — LAW II).
37	
38	Pure leaf module: no network, no model import, no faithfulness-engine import. Two env
39	kill-switches (LAW VI), DEFAULT ON. When the corpus has zero junk / off-topic rows the
40	groundable pool is byte-identical to the input.
41	"""

112	def is_row_content_junk(row: Mapping[str, Any]) -> bool:
113	    """True iff this row is a chrome non-source (bot / cookie / 404 / login / empty).
114	
115	    Reads a pre-existing ``content_integrity_junk`` stamp first (cheap), and when the stamp
116	    is ABSENT runs the pure leaf detector on the row itself. The stamp is only written by the
117	    fetch-path fold-in (``outline/_fold_in.py``), so a run fed a PRE-BUILT corpus carries no
118	    stamp at all — and a stamp-only predicate then fails open on every row, silently reducing
119	    this gate to a no-op exactly when it is the only thing standing between a failed fetch and
120	    the grounding pool. Falling back to the detector makes the gate independent of WHO
121	    assembled the corpus.

124	    same leaf detector, so both chrome screens judge the SAME body. It deliberately does NOT call

126	    the detector's CLASS to apply the ``empty`` fence below. The two screens can therefore differ

128	    SKIPS it) and NOT chrome here (where the verdict DELETES). That asymmetry is deliberate and is
129	    the fail-open direction: the screen with the destructive verdict is the stricter one.

132	    bot challenge / cookie wall / 404 / login wall / non-article stub). It deliberately does
133	    NOT honour the detector's ``empty`` class, which fires when a row has no title and no body
134	    in any KNOWN text field. On the fetch path "empty" is a real verdict (the fetch returned
135	    nothing); at the POOL it only means the text was not where this module looked — a row that
136	    keeps its prose under an unfamiliar key would then be deleted for the predicate's own
137	    ignorance, which is exactly the false hard-drop §-1.3 forbids. Absence of evidence is not
138	    a chrome signature. A row STAMPED by the fetch path still deletes on any class (that
139	    caller genuinely saw the empty body).

142	    NEVER delete a source."""

172	    """True iff a SEMANTIC topic judge confirmed this WHOLE source off-topic to the
173	    research question. Reuses ``weighted_enrichment._is_confirmed_offtopic`` — the single

179	            _is_confirmed_offtopic,

183	        logger.warning(
184	            "[junk_deletion_gate] offtopic predicate errored (%s) — row KEPT "

189	
190	def _stamped_off_subject(row: Mapping[str, Any]) -> bool:
191	    """True iff the topic judge stamped this WHOLE source OFF_SUBJECT — a clearly different
192	    subject (the DELETABLE class of the OFF split, e.g. a scholar-mill / tourism / religion
193	    page in an AI-labor report). The Fix-3 sidecar is the bool ``topic_off_subject=True``; a
194	    string verdict ``topic_relevance_verdict == "OFF_SUBJECT"`` is tolerated as the same
195	    signal. Legacy ``topic_offtopic_demoted`` alone (which conflates OFF_ASPECT 'same entity,
196	    wrong aspect' with OFF_SUBJECT) does NOT count — a demote-keep aspect verdict is never a
197	    delete trigger, so only the explicit subject-level stamp deletes."""
198	    v = row.get("topic_off_subject")
199	    if v is True:
200	        return True
201	    if isinstance(v, str) and v.strip().lower() in _OFF_SUBJECT_TRUE_TOKENS:
202	        return True
203	    verdict = str(row.get("topic_relevance_verdict", "") or "").strip().lower()
204	    return verdict == "off_subject"
205	
206	
207	def is_row_deletable_offtopic(
208	    row: Mapping[str, Any],
209	    fresh_off_subject_ids: "Iterable[str] | None" = None,
210	) -> bool:
211	    """DELETE predicate for the confirmed-off-topic class (topic-judge-only path, Fix 1 + Fix 2).
212	
213	    Returns True (row is DELETABLE) iff the topic judge AFFIRMATIVELY stamped this WHOLE
214	    source OFF_SUBJECT. Three hard guarantees keep this from over-deleting:
215	
216	    * A weight/reranker ``content_relevance_label`` (``demoted`` / ``escalated_demoted``) is
217	      NEVER a delete trigger — those are weight-demote-to-0.25 KEEP labels (a Qwen3-Reranker
218	      numeric score / a GLM 'INSUFFICIENT' entailment), NOT topic verdicts. Reusing them as a
219	      DELETE trigger is exactly what hard-deleted 197 on-topic rows (St. Louis Fed, OECD, ILO,
220	      McKinsey, HBS, Wikipedia, 23 T1 journal papers) in the I-deepfix-003 pass.
221	    * An affirmative positive-relevance verdict (``content_relevance_label`` in
222	      {relevant, escalated_relevant}) VETOES deletion UNCONDITIONALLY — even against a
223	      stale/false OFF stamp. When the two judges disagree, the positive relevance wins.
224	    * Fix 2 (fresh-verdict-only): when ``fresh_off_subject_ids`` is supplied (a concrete set —
225	      the evidence_ids THIS run's topic judge freshly confirmed OFF_SUBJECT) AND
226	      ``PG_DELETE_OFFTOPIC_FRESH_VERDICT_ONLY`` is ON, a row whose OFF_SUBJECT stamp is NOT in
227	      that set is a STALE snapshot stamp (an earlier run's verdict reloaded from
228	      corpus_snapshot) and demote-KEEPs — only a fresh verdict deletes. ``None`` (the default)
229	      => freshness is un-enforced (byte-identical to the Fix-1-only path).
230	
231	    FAIL-OPEN: not a Mapping / no OFF_SUBJECT stamp / stale-only stamp / missing verdict / any
232	    error => NOT deletable (row KEPT). A predicate bug must NEVER delete a source."""
233	    try:
234	        if not isinstance(row, Mapping):
235	            return False
236	        label = str(row.get("content_relevance_label", "") or "").strip().lower()
237	        if label in _POSITIVE_RELEVANCE_LABELS:
238	            return False  # affirmative positive-relevance verdict — KEEP (unconditional veto)
239	        if not _stamped_off_subject(row):
240	            return False
241	        # Fix 2: a STALE snapshot OFF_SUBJECT stamp (not in this run's fresh set) demote-KEEPs.
242	        # Enforced only when the caller passes a concrete set AND the flag is ON.
243	        if fresh_off_subject_ids is not None and fresh_verdict_only_deletion_enabled():
244	            eid = str(row.get("evidence_id", "") or "")
245	            if eid not in {str(e) for e in fresh_off_subject_ids}:
246	                return False  # stale stamp — demote-keep, never delete on an old verdict
247	        return True
248	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — never delete on a predicate bug
249	        logger.warning(
250	            "[junk_deletion_gate] deletable-offtopic predicate errored (%s) — row KEPT "
251	            "(fail-open).", str(exc)[:160],
252	        )

255	
256	def partition_rows(
257	    rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
258	    exempt_ids: "Iterable[str] | None" = None,
259	    fresh_off_subject_ids: "Iterable[str] | None" = None,
260	) -> tuple[list[dict[str, Any]], list[dict[str, Any]]]:
261	    """Split the run-level grounding pool (``evidence_for_gen``) into
262	    ``(kept, deleted)``.
263	
264	    A row is DELETED iff it is a chrome non-source (kill-switch A) OR a
265	    confirmed-off-topic source (kill-switch B), AND its ``evidence_id`` is NOT in
266	    ``exempt_ids`` (a marquee / contract-bound anchor is on-topic by construction and is
267	    never deleted). Each deleted row carries a ``deletion_reason`` for disclosure.
268	
269	    Order-preserving; never mutates the input rows (a deleted row is copied before the
270	    reason is stamped); idempotent (re-running on a clean pool returns it unchanged with
271	    an empty deleted list). When BOTH kill-switches are OFF, returns ``(list(rows), [])``
272	    — byte-identical.
273	    """
274	    chrome_on = chrome_deletion_enabled()
275	    offtopic_on = offtopic_deletion_enabled()
276	    if not chrome_on and not offtopic_on:
277	        return list(rows), []
278	    exempt = {str(e) for e in (exempt_ids or ())}
279	    # Fix 2: materialize the fresh OFF_SUBJECT id set ONCE (or None to leave freshness
280	    # un-enforced). A stale-stamp row whose id is absent demote-KEEPs (see
281	    # is_row_deletable_offtopic).
282	    fresh_ids = None if fresh_off_subject_ids is None else {str(e) for e in fresh_off_subject_ids}
283	    kept: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
284	    deleted: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
285	    for row in rows:
286	        if not isinstance(row, Mapping):
287	            kept.append(row)
288	            continue
289	        eid = str(row.get("evidence_id", "") or "")
290	        if eid and eid in exempt:
291	            kept.append(row)  # marquee / contract anchor — NEVER deleted
292	            continue
293	        reason = ""
294	        if chrome_on and is_row_content_junk(row):
295	            reason = "content_integrity_junk:" + str(
296	                row.get("content_integrity_class", "chrome") or "chrome"
297	            )
298	        elif offtopic_on:
299	            # Fix 1: default-ON path deletes ONLY on the topic judge's OFF_SUBJECT stamp
300	            # (weight labels can never delete; positive relevance vetoes). The legacy
301	            # weight-label predicate stays reachable ONLY behind the OFF kill-switch so
302	            # ``PG_DELETE_OFFTOPIC_TOPIC_JUDGE_ONLY=0`` is byte-identical to pre-Fix-1.
303	            if topic_judge_only_deletion_enabled():
304	                if is_row_deletable_offtopic(row, fresh_off_subject_ids=fresh_ids):
305	                    reason = "confirmed_offtopic_subject"
306	            elif is_row_confirmed_offtopic(row):
307	                reason = "confirmed_offtopic"
308	        if reason:
309	            copied = dict(row)
310	            copied["deletion_reason"] = reason
311	            deleted.append(copied)
312	        else:
313	            kept.append(row)
314	    return kept, deleted
315	
316	
317	def _deletion_signal(reason: str) -> str:
318	    """Human-readable 'which signal fired' derived from the deletion_reason — a chrome class
319	    or the topic-judge OFF_SUBJECT stamp. Keeps the disclosure honest about the exact
320	    category (chrome non-source vs confirmed-off-topic) that removed each row."""
321	    if reason.startswith("content_integrity_junk"):
322	        cls = reason.split(":", 1)[1] if ":" in reason else "chrome"
323	        return "chrome:" + (cls or "chrome")
324	    if reason.startswith("confirmed_offtopic"):
325	        return "topic_judge_off_subject"
326	    return reason or "unknown"
327	
328	
329	def _judge_verdict_summary(row: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str:
330	    """Compact per-source verdict trail the row carried at deletion time (read-only — never a
331	    delete trigger here). Surfaces WHY the source was judged, so a reviewer can second-guess
332	    any deletion from the manifest alone."""
333	    parts: list[str] = []
334	    if _stamped_off_subject(row):
335	        parts.append("topic=OFF_SUBJECT")
336	    if row.get("topic_offtopic_demoted") is True:
337	        parts.append("topic_offtopic_demoted=True")
338	    label = str(row.get("content_relevance_label", "") or "").strip()
339	    if label:
340	        parts.append("content_relevance_label=" + label)
341	    verdict = str(row.get("topic_relevance_verdict", "") or "").strip()
342	    if verdict:
343	        parts.append("topic_relevance_verdict=" + verdict)
344	    ci_class = str(row.get("content_integrity_class", "") or "").strip()
345	    if ci_class:
346	        parts.append("content_integrity_class=" + ci_class)
347	    return ";".join(parts)
348	
349	
350	def disclosure_records(deleted_rows: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
351	    """Compact disclosure rows for the run telemetry — the audit trail that proves the
352	    pipeline found and DELETED each junk / off-topic source (recorded, never silent).
353	
354	    Fix 5 (records half): each record now carries the per-source SIGNAL that fired
355	    (chrome class vs topic-judge OFF_SUBJECT), the JUDGE VERDICT trail, and the source TIER
356	    alongside the existing evidence_id / title / url / deletion_reason — enough to second-
357	    guess any single deletion straight from the manifest (fail-loud, never silent)."""
358	    out: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
359	    for row in deleted_rows:
360	        if not isinstance(row, Mapping):
361	            continue
362	        reason = str(row.get("deletion_reason", "") or "")
363	        out.append({
364	            "evidence_id": row.get("evidence_id", ""),
365	            "title": row.get("source_title") or row.get("title") or "",
366	            "url": row.get("url") or row.get("source_url") or "",
367	            "tier": row.get("tier") or row.get("source_tier") or "",
368	            "deletion_reason": reason,
369	            "signal": _deletion_signal(reason),
370	            "judge_verdict": _judge_verdict_summary(row),
371	            "excluded_from_grounding": True,
372	        })
373	    return out

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""STEP 15: compose the REAL scoreable multi-section report from the LIVE agentic run.
3	
4	The mission's metric-(a) full-corpus gate (cp4_used=agentic on the 329-basket corpus) already
5	PASSED (docs/agentic_sweep_live_summary_s3gear329.json). What was missing was a *composed*
6	report we can score. This driver closes that gap: it runs the FULL generator
7	(``generate_multi_section_report``) with the agentic outliner ON (PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1 + the
8	§9.1.8 model lock) over data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json and writes report.md.
9	
10	Model it on scripts/run_honest_on_prerebuild_corpus.py (which already produced report.md +
11	multi_section_outline.json), minus the retrieval/scope machinery (the corpus is pre-built).
12	
13	Faithfulness gate (HARD): after composition, assert ZERO unverified numbers reach any
14	[CITE:ev_xxx] token in the composed report. The strict_verify lane already enforces this
15	per-section; this driver re-audits the final assembled text as an independent tripwire.
16	
17	Run (key MUST be in env):
18	    set -a && . ./.env && set +a
19	    PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1 python scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py \
20	        --corpus data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json
21	"""
22	from __future__ import annotations
23	
24	import argparse
25	import asyncio
26	import json
27	import logging
28	import os
29	import re
30	import sys
31	import time
32	from pathlib import Path
33	from types import SimpleNamespace
34	
35	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
36	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))
37	
38	DRB_QUERY = ROOT / "third_party" / "deep_research_bench" / "data" / "prompt_data" / "query.jsonl"
39	
40	# STEP 2 (wheel: topic-driven structure) — the section headings are now produced TOPIC-DRIVEN by
41	# the generator itself (facet outline + general research-report skeleton: PG_FACET_OUTLINE=1 +
42	# PG_FACET_OUTLINE_SKELETON=1). The prior STEP-16 approach hardcoded a clinical-archetype ->
43	# AI/labor relabel MAP here — an overfit band-aid tuned to one benchmark task. That map is GONE:
44	# the outliner emits real topical titles (Introduction / thematic bodies / Cross-Study Synthesis /
45	# Conclusions and Research Gaps) for ANY domain, so assembly renders the section titles verbatim.
46	
47	
48	def _derive_title(rq: str) -> str:
49	    """Derive a neutral report title from the research question — GENERAL, not tuned to any task.
50	
51	    Takes the first sentence/clause of the RQ, strips a leading imperative ("Please write a ...",
52	    "Research ...", "I am researching ..."), and Title-cases nothing (keeps the RQ's own wording).
53	    Falls back to a generic label. No topic is hardcoded."""
54	    import re as _re
55	    s = (rq or "").strip().replace("\n", " ")
56	    s = _re.sub(r"\s+", " ", s)
57	    # First sentence only.
58	    s = _re.split(r"(?<=[.?!])\s", s, maxsplit=1)[0]
59	    # Strip common leading imperatives so the title reads as a subject, not a command.
60	    s = _re.sub(r"^(please\s+)?(help me\s+)?(write|prepare|produce|conduct|research(ing)?|"
61	                r"provide|create|complete|collect( and)?( organi[sz]e)?|i am researching|"
62	                r"i would like|i need)\b[:,]?\s*", "", s, flags=_re.IGNORECASE)
63	    s = s.strip().rstrip(".").strip()
64	    if not s:
65	        return "Research Report"
66	    # Capitalize the first letter only (preserve proper-noun casing in the rest).
67	    return s[0].upper() + s[1:]
68	
69	
70	def _load_drb_prompt(task_id: str) -> str:
71	    """Load a DeepResearch-Bench task's EXACT prompt verbatim (target/ref/criteria all key on it)."""
72	    for line in DRB_QUERY.read_text().splitlines():
73	        o = json.loads(line)
74	        if str(o.get("id")) == str(task_id):
75	            return o["prompt"]
76	    raise SystemExit(f"BLOCKED: DRB task id {task_id} not in {DRB_QUERY}")
77	
78	logging.basicConfig(
79	    level=os.environ.get("PG_LOG_LEVEL", "INFO"),
80	    format="%(asctime)s %(name)s %(levelname)s %(message)s",
81	    datefmt="%H:%M:%S",
82	    stream=sys.stdout,
83	)
84	for noisy in ("httpx", "httpcore"):
85	    logging.getLogger(noisy).setLevel(logging.WARNING)
86	log = logging.getLogger("compose")
87	
88	
89	def _tier_fractions(evidence: list[dict]) -> dict[str, float]:
90	    from collections import Counter
91	    c = Counter((e.get("tier") or "T?").upper() for e in evidence)
92	    n = sum(c.values()) or 1
93	    return {k: v / n for k, v in sorted(c.items())}
94	
95	
96	# A numeric token that would be a faithfulness breach if it sat inside a [CITE:] sentence
97	# without having passed strict_verify. We audit the FINAL assembled report: any [CITE:ev_xxx]
98	# in the verified text is, by construction, already span-grounded — but we re-scan to prove it.
99	_CITE_RE = re.compile(r"\[CITE:(ev_[0-9a-fA-F]+|[a-z0-9_]+)\]")
100	
101	
102	def _audit_citations(report_text: str, biblio: list[dict]) -> dict:
103	    """Independent faithfulness tripwire on the FINAL assembled report.
104	
105	    strict_verify resolves every kept sentence's provenance token into a global [N] bibliography
106	    marker and DROPS any sentence whose number failed the span match. So in a faithful final
107	    report: (1) ZERO raw [CITE:ev_xxx] tokens survive (any survivor is an unverified-number leak
108	    — the exact breach the mission forbids), and (2) every [N] marker in the prose resolves to a
109	    real bibliography entry. We assert both."""
110	    leaked_cites = _CITE_RE.findall(report_text)
111	    body = report_text.split("\n\n## References\n", 1)[0]  # markers in prose only
112	    n_markers = set(int(m) for m in re.findall(r"\[(\d+)\]", body))
113	    biblio_nums = {int(b.get("num")) for b in biblio if str(b.get("num", "")).isdigit()}
114	    unresolved = sorted(n for n in n_markers if n not in biblio_nums)
115	    return {
116	        "leaked_cite_ev_tokens": len(leaked_cites),
117	        "leaked_cite_samples": sorted(set(leaked_cites))[:10],
118	        "distinct_bib_markers_in_prose": len(n_markers),
119	        "bibliography_entries": len(biblio_nums),
120	        "unresolved_markers": unresolved,
121	    }
122	
123	
124	async def main() -> int:
125	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
126	    ap.add_argument("--corpus", required=True)
127	    ap.add_argument("--out-dir", default=None)
128	    ap.add_argument("--max-parallel", type=int, default=3)
129	    ap.add_argument("--rq-drb-task", default="72",
130	                    help="override the corpus RQ with this DRB task's verbatim prompt so the "
131	                         "composed report answers the SAME task it is scored against; empty string "
132	                         "keeps the corpus RQ")
133	    ap.add_argument("--title", default=None,
134	                    help="report title for the judged report.md; default DERIVES it from the RQ "
135	                         "(general — no title is hardcoded to any task)")
136	    args = ap.parse_args()
137	
138	    if not os.getenv("OPENROUTER_API_KEY"):
139	        log.error("BLOCKED: OPENROUTER_API_KEY not in env — source .env first "
140	                  "(set -a && . ./.env && set +a)")
141	        return 2
142	    # The mission model-lock: agentic outliner ON.
143	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_OUTLINE_AGENT", "1")
144	    # P0 CONFIRMED-SAFE COMPOSE CONFIG (2026-07-12) — PIN the non-deadlocking config in the launch
145	    # path. The clean 24.2min/1449.7s run used exactly this: off-loop ON (shipped, verdict-safe),
146	    # PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS=1 (serial byte-identical MAP+REDUCE — NEVER >1 without a full-328
147	    # verdict-identity A/B), PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY in the 4-8 band (NEVER >=48), and
148	    # PG_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=3. These are setdefault (an explicit operator override still wins) but they
149	    # keep this driver on the certified-safe path; the startup guard (compose_config_guard) refuses the
150	    # deadlocking regime regardless. Faithfulness-neutral: pure concurrency knobs.
151	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS", "1")
152	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY", "8")
153	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_PARALLEL_SECTIONS", "3")
154	    # P1-SPEED (2026-07-12) — collapse the ISOLATED pre-compose credibility member-verify pass.
155	    # ROOT-CAUSE of the 43min (2589.7s) >> 24min (1449.7s) gap, MEASURED from the phase timeline in
156	    # logs/step3_full328_render.log: threading the PSL gov_suffixes (below) to lift route_all basket
157	    # utilization ALSO activates the ADVISORY credibility corroboration pass. On this 997-member corpus
158	    # that pass ran SERIALLY (PG_CREDIBILITY_PASS_MAX_INFLIGHT default=1) and BANKED at its
159	    # wall*0.85 soft deadline = 1020s, verifying only 207/997 members — a full +1020s phase the 1449.7s
160	    # baseline NEVER ran (it did not thread gov_suffixes -> credibility degraded-to-unscored, skipped).
161	    # This pass runs ENTIRELY BEFORE compose (an ISOLATED flat phase — NO PG_PARALLEL_SECTIONS x
162	    # PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS x inner-TPE nesting), so bounding its OWN loop concurrency is NOT the
163	    # multiplicative compose oversubscription the deadlock guard protects against. Parallelize the
164	    # member-verify loop and raise the side-judge cap FOR THIS PHASE ONLY (the designed I-deepfix-001
165	    # box2 lever; credibility_pass_concurrency RESTORES the compose-time cap of 8 before compose starts).
166	    # Faithfulness-neutral & UNDERCOUNT-only: the pass is ADVISORY (strict_verify / 4-role D8 /
167	    # span-grounding are untouched); verifying MORE members in LESS time yields STRICTLY MORE
168	    # corroboration than the 207-serial run and far more than the baseline's zero. All env-overridable.
169	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_CREDIBILITY_PASS_MAX_INFLIGHT", "16")
170	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_CREDIBILITY_PASS_SIDE_JUDGE_CONCURRENCY", "16")
171	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_CREDIBILITY_PASS_WALL_S", "600")
172	    # STEP 2: topic-driven, synthesis-enabling structure. Facet outline (thematic sections emerge
173	    # from the evidence) + the general research-report skeleton (intro / thematic bodies /
174	    # cross-study synthesis+contradictions / conclusions+gaps). GENERAL structural flags — they
175	    # hardcode no topic and are overridable from the environment.
176	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_FACET_OUTLINE", "1")
177	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_FACET_OUTLINE_SKELETON", "1")
178	    # STEP 3 (INSIGHT depth): make the cross-study synthesis section quantify agreement/disagreement
179	    # across the [ev]-backed body figures (enrich its evidence + directive). GENERAL structural
180	    # lever — role detected structurally, no topic/title hardcoded; strict_verify unchanged.
181	    os.environ.setdefault("PG_SYNTHESIS_QUANT_DIRECTIVE", "1")
182	    # STEP 4 (UTILIZATION — the basket under-utilization ghost): the live LLM outline lists only a
183	    # handful of ev_ids per section, so ~90% of the consolidated baskets reach NO section and never
184	    # compose a cited claim (measured 31/329 rendered; scripts/measure_utilization_route_all.py). Route
185	    # every ORPHAN basket to its best-matching thematic section by claim-vs-title content overlap (else a

105	# I-deepfix-001 (#1344) DEFER-1 — off-topic CITE-SURFACE suppression.
106	#
107	# drb_72 cited OFF-TOPIC sources (B1 supply-chain blogs, B2 Russian cosmetics, B3
108	# recycling journal, B4 post-merger M&A) as numbered findings in the breadth
109	# "Corroborated Weighted Findings" section. The keystone (I-arch-011 B18) correctly
110	# forbids the lexical ``selection_relevance < floor`` DROP (it killed 729/746 real
111	# unbound SUPPORTS and also suppressed REAL on-topic low-cred sources). So the
112	# discriminator is NOT the noisy score — it is the SEMANTIC confirmed-OFF verdict
113	# the topic gate / W2 content-relevance judge already produced and stamped on the
114	# row (``topic_offtopic_demoted`` / ``content_relevance_label``). A confirmed
115	# "this is about cosmetics, not AI labor" verdict is, by definition, not a
116	# corroborator of an AI-labor claim, so withholding it from CITATION is not a
117	# §-1.3 hard-drop: the source STAYS in evidence_pool + the credibility disclosure
118	# (kept-and-disclosed). Gated default-ON; ``PG_OFFTOPIC_CITE_SUPPRESS=0`` restores
119	# the byte-identical legacy cite-surface.
120	_CONFIRMED_OFFTOPIC_LABELS = frozenset({"demoted", "escalated_demoted"})
121	
122	
123	def offtopic_cite_suppress_enabled() -> bool:
124	    """Kill-switch ``PG_OFFTOPIC_CITE_SUPPRESS`` (default ON). OFF => the cite
125	    surface is byte-identical to the legacy keep-all-and-cite behaviour."""
126	    return os.environ.get("PG_OFFTOPIC_CITE_SUPPRESS", "1").strip().lower() not in (
127	        "0", "false", "no", "off",
128	    )

131	_POSITIVE_RELEVANCE_LABELS = frozenset({"relevant", "escalated_relevant"})
132	
133	
134	def _offtopic_relevance_override_enabled() -> bool:
135	    """Kill-switch ``PG_OFFTOPIC_RELEVANCE_OVERRIDE`` (default ON). OFF => the
136	    legacy byte-identical ``_is_confirmed_offtopic`` (topic-flag wins)."""
137	    return os.environ.get("PG_OFFTOPIC_RELEVANCE_OVERRIDE", "1").strip().lower() not in (
138	        "0", "false", "no", "off",
139	    )

142	def _is_confirmed_offtopic(row: Any) -> bool:
143	    """True iff a SEMANTIC judge confirmed this source is OFF-topic.
144	
145	    Keys ONLY on the topic-gate sidecar (``topic_offtopic_demoted is True``) OR the
146	    W2 content-relevance LABEL (``content_relevance_label`` in
147	    {``demoted``, ``escalated_demoted``}) — NEVER on the noisy lexical/embedding
148	    ``selection_relevance`` score (that is the §-1.3-banned keystone DROP). A
149	    missing/absent label is keep-neutral (NOT off-topic): an unjudged or
150	    judged-RELEVANT row is never suppressed.
151	
152	    I-deepfix-001 (drb_72 forensic): when the TWO semantic judges DISAGREE — the
153	    W2 content-relevance judge affirmatively rated the source ``relevant`` /
154	    ``escalated_relevant`` (high weight) while the topic gate stamped
155	    ``topic_offtopic_demoted=True`` — the AFFIRMATIVE relevance verdict WINS and the
156	    source is NOT suppressed. This is the §-1.3 weight-not-filter rule: a false-positive
157	    off-topic flag must never bury a source the content-relevance judge judged relevant.
158	    (The drb_72 corpus buried the SEMINAL papers this way — GPTs-are-GPTs / World Bank /
159	    Humlum all carried content_relevance_label='relevant' weight=1.0 BUT
160	    topic_offtopic_demoted=True, so the old predicate suppressed them from the finding
161	    surface.) Behind ``PG_OFFTOPIC_RELEVANCE_OVERRIDE`` (default ON); OFF = byte-identical."""
162	    if not isinstance(row, dict):
163	        return False
164	    label = str(row.get("content_relevance_label", "") or "").strip().lower()
165	    if _offtopic_relevance_override_enabled() and label in _POSITIVE_RELEVANCE_LABELS:
166	        # judges conflict (relevant vs off-topic-demoted) -> trust the positive relevance, KEEP.
167	        return False
168	    if row.get("topic_offtopic_demoted") is True:
169	        return True
170	    return label in _CONFIRMED_OFFTOPIC_LABELS
